title: Constant Contact vs Sendinblue (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Constant Contact and Sendinblue. See pricing, features, ratings, and find the best email marketing tool for your needs.
h1: Constant Contact vs Sendinblue: Detailed Comparison
intro: Constant Contact and Sendinblue (now Brevo) are two established email marketing platforms with different strengths. Constant Contact focuses on local businesses and nonprofits with event management and social media features, while Sendinblue excels at transactional email and offers a generous free tier. Understanding their differences will help you choose the right platform for your marketing needs.

comparison_table.tool_a: Nonprofits and local businesses with e-commerce needs
comparison_table.tool_b: Transactional email, SMS campaigns, and CRM integration

comparison_table.tool_a: Event management, social media ads, e-commerce, SMS
comparison_table.tool_b: Free tier, transactional API, WhatsApp, chat, CRM

choose_a_if: You need event management and social media ad integration for local business marketing
You prioritize e-commerce features and email templates designed for online stores
You want dedicated support for nonprofits with nonprofit-specific features
choose_b_if: You want to start free with up to 100,000 subscribers and 300 daily emails
You need transactional email capabilities and API integration for developers
You prefer an all-in-one CRM suite with WhatsApp and chat functionality included

faq.q: Is Constant Contact better than Sendinblue?
faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your needs. Sendinblue has higher customer satisfaction ratings (4.5 on G2 vs 4.0) and a free tier, making it better for budget-conscious users. Constant Contact excels at event management and e-commerce features, making it superior for local businesses and online retailers.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Constant Contact or Sendinblue?
faq.a: Sendinblue is cheaper overall because it offers a free tier supporting up to 100,000 subscribers with 300 emails per day. Constant Contact has no free option and starts at $12/month. If you need paid plans, Sendinblue's paid tier begins at $25/month compared to Constant Contact's $12/month, but the free option makes Sendinblue the better value for startups.

faq.q: Can I switch from Sendinblue to Constant Contact?
faq.a: Yes, you can migrate between platforms. Both tools allow you to export your contact lists and email templates. Most migration can be done through CSV exports, though you may lose some advanced automation sequences during the switch. Plan the migration carefully to minimize downtime in your email campaigns.
